steps.title: Find the number of doubling periods
steps.content: From 1652 to 1952, the time elapsed is 1952 − 1652 = 300 years. Since the population doubles every 75 years, the number of doubling periods is 300 ÷ 75 = 4.

steps.title: Calculate the initial population
steps.content:
When a population doubles 4 times, it multiplies by $2^4 = 16$. If $P_0$ is the population in 1652:

$$P_0 \times 2^4 = 160,000$$
$$P_0 \times 16 = 160,000$$
$$P_0 = \frac{160,000}{16} = 10,000$$

The population in 1652 was 10,000.
